mission.title: container-network-interface-cni-: converting plugin result to wrong config version results in regression
mission.description: converting plugin result to wrong config version results in regression . Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current container-network-interface-cni- deployment
steps.description:
Verify your container-network-interface-cni- version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n container-network-interface-cni- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=container-network-interface-cni-
helm list -n container-network-interface-cni- 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working container-network-interface-cni- installation.

steps.title: Review container-network-interface-cni- configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant container-network-interface-cni- configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n container-network-interface-cni- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=container-network-interface-cni-
kubectl get configmap -n container-network-interface-cni- -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=container-network-interface-cni-
```
https://github.com/weaveworks/weave/issues/3936
https://github.com/containerd/containerd/issues/6921

 drafted a prototype fix:
https://github.com/fuweid/containerd/commit/8c5607ca4edae3bfd21a15f7d3f9db2d17fee0f0

steps.title: Apply the fix for converting plugin result to wrong config version results in…
steps.description:
The CNI Spec requires plugins to return a CNIVersion in the Result
that is the same as the CNIVersion of the incoming CNI config.

Empty CNIVersions are specified to map to 0.1.0 (since the first
CNI spec didn't have versioning) and libcni handles that automatically.

Unfortunately some versions of Weave don't do that and depend on
a libcni <= 0.8 quirk that used the netconf version if the result
version was empty. This is technically a libcni regression, though
the plugin itself is violating

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Upgrade container-network-interface-cni- to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade container-network-interface-cni-:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade container-network-interface-cni- container-network-interface-cni-/container-network-interface-cni- --namespace container-network-interface-cni-
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n container-network-interface-cni-
helm list -n container-network-interface-cni-
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n container-network-interface-cni- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=container-network-interface-cni-
kubectl get events -n container-network-interface-cni- --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "converting plugin result to wrong config version results in…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
The CNI Spec requires plugins to return a CNIVersion in the Result
that is the same as the CNIVersion of the incoming CNI config.

Empty CNIVersions are specified to map to 0.1.0 (since the first
CNI spec didn't have versioning) and libcni handles that automatically.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with container-network-interface-cni- installed or the issue environment reproducible.
